hotel_name: Khách sạn Ibis Saigon South (Ibis Saigon South Hotel)

reviews.title: Hotel manager removed all my belonging after 12pm
reviews.text: I tried to check out the hotel at 1230pm, however I was shock to find out my room was opened and all my belonging gone! The hotel removed my bags and all toiletary after I failed to check out by 12pm. Hotel manager claim this is IBIS policy. I have never encounter this in my life and I will never book Ibis again!
reviews.response:
Dear Lim, 
Thank you for your time to sharing the experience at Ibis Saigon South. I was sad to hear that your stay experience was not meet your expectation and disappointing about our staff attitude. We will take this lesson seriously to learn and re-train Hotel Team about Customer service. Your feedback is very valuable and much appreciated for us to realize the problem and trive to provide more pleasant service. Again, we would like to express our sincere apology for your dissatisfied experience with us.
However, we are still looking forward to welcoming you again in near future with better service.
Yours sincerely, 
Rooms Division Manager

reviews.title: Wouldn’t recommend as I couldn’t even get much sleep in.
reviews.positives: Good community and clean.
reviews.negatives: Did not warn it was across the street from a music festival when booking. The concert went to 1130PM (from 10am), it was so loud the whole room was vibrating. Then the power went out for over 30 minutes after. Staff told us elevator was working just fine, as soon as we got in it shut down and we were stuck. By the morning there was no hot water. Horrible experience.

reviews.title: Regret booking and I do not recommend
reviews.text: Arrived  tired from Nha Trang and my tour with the Movie Documentary Crew who just flew home.  The street in front is under construction and must walk up two wet metal ramps just to get up to the bricks laid in mud to get to the door--Don't expect any help with luggage because its not coming.  Just getting into the shower when a extremely loud fire alarm go's off and a message is loudly being broadcast over loud speakers about a fire alarm being activated and an unintelligible message going for about 5 mins. My head hurt from the noise.  Later again while on an international 3 way Business call and again the alarm go off ruining my call.  The staff could have warned guest a test would be done and acted offended that I complained . My long time friend a lovely well dressed lady who works in medical supply, dropped by to see me and go to dinner and the staff was very rude to her and acted very snoty.  The room is like a closet with a bed and a hard chair. Surprised this is IBIS property.
reviews.response:
Dear Mr Ronie, 

Thank you for your staying with us and taking your time to review. We regret that our service was not meet your expectation. The street is under  construction and managed by our local Government.
We have been under going the full hotel alarm system to ensure our guest safety is not compromised. We had set up announcement letter in the room and we apologies that this was not cleared communicated to you. We also take note on your feedback about our team member attitude and arrange for training. 

 We thank you once again for your time and sharing your concern. We hope you have quality time and enjoy your trip in Saigon, despite rather inconvenient experience, and wish to serve you with improvement in the future. 

Sincerely, 

Kathy

Rooms Division Manager

reviews.title: Besides the location near SECC, nothing good.
reviews.text:
This is my first time giving poor review for hotel. So sorry to tell that I will not come back in future. 
Hotel looks okay, but NOT clean. I was welcome with dirty bath towel, and cockroaches in my room. There was a bunch of hairs inside the folded bath towel on the very first day. Most of the provided bath towel having stain on it also.  Total of 4 cockroaches I killed during my stay. 
On the other hand, the hotel has no added facilities like gym room, nor swimming pool. Very basic, just for sleep. 
WiFi internet speed also considered slow and need improvement.
reviews.response:
Dear Mr Wei, 

Thank you for taking the time to tell us about your experience. I am sorry that your experience did not meet your expectations; please allow me to express my sincerest apologies. 

We are addressing this issues and discussing with our management team to ensure that we are taking the appropriate measures to improve and prevent from happening again. 

We appreciate sharing your concerns, and it is our hope that you will give us the opportunity to better serve you in the near future.

Yours sincerely, 

Kathy Nguyen

Rooms Division Manager

The lady staff is helpful to exchange for me, however, I think the hotel have to provide some value added.

reviews.title: Functional business hotel, lacks warmth.
reviews.text:
My wife and I booked into the Ibis for New Years, in order to avoid the traffic and crowds of District One. Although the hotel is new, clean and comfortable, we were disappointed with the service. 

Upon arrival the reception staff demanded a copy of my marriage licence, to prove that my wife and I are married. I had to travel across town to my home to pick it up. The communication was very poor. They spoke in Vietnamese to my wife, effectively ignoring me and excluding me from the conversation. This is extremely rude considering that the booking was in my name. My wife reports that the way they spoke to her was rude. 

If the Ibis is going to adhere to this discriminatory policy, they should make it known to prospective guests. Had I known this would be an issue I would have booked into one of many hotels that I know will use their discretion and common sense. 

Ibis also demanded a cash deposit for "incidental charges". I have never been asked to pay a deposit in any hotel in any country. They backed down when I refused, but it added to what was a very cold reception. 

We were also not allowed to have dinner in the restaurant at 5pm (we had tickets to a movie at 7pm) because there would be a New Years buffet at 7pm. 

All in all, we felt unwelcome and judged by the staff of this hotel. 

We chose the hotel for it's quiet location near to the Crescent mall and restaurants. If you have business in District 3 the location is perfect. If you're a tourist or will be spending you're better off staying in D1.

reviews.positives: Единственный плюс - удобное расположение. Совсем рядом с выставочным центром.
reviews.negatives:
Не понравилось все. 
Начиная с того, что при заселении выдали талоны на завтрак, но почему-то только одному из 2 проживающих. Второму пришлось утром спускаться вниз и доказывать, что они имеет право на оплаченный завтрак.
Завтраки - худшие в моей жизни. Скудно, не вкусно. Чай - это заваренный в литровом чайнике 1(!!!) чайный пакетик. Если уж здесь и останавливаться, то точно за завтрак платить не стоит.

При заселении взимают депозит. Зачем, если нет мини-бара? Забирать в отеле точно нечего, можно только свое что-то оставить...

В ванной сильнейшая  плесень.

В тесном номере для 2-х человек почему-то тапочки и пр. только для одного.

Лифты - отдельная тема. Надо выходить заранее, чтобы дождаться его.

В общем - сплошное разочарование.

Ибису должно быть стыдно за такой  сетевой отель.

reviews.positives: Location was okay....
reviews.negatives:
There was a blackout every night of the four nights I stayed there. First night blackout lasted 20-30 mins. Even the lift broke down and couldn't be used. The staff were nice but seemed like it's a common occurrence. They seemed more concerned about the issue being under 15 min due to the Ibis policy, then actually being apologetic about the inconvenience this was around 1am on 8th Dec. Come to think of it the hotel wasn't apologetic at all.

Successive days, 9/10/11 blackouts occurred around 3-5am. This woke me up because the aircon cuts off.

reviews.text: The stay was ok. My room was small but very clean. TV reception was good as was the internet. I checked in with my fiancé, which presented a problem as we did not have a wedding certificate, something the hotel frowned upon. This is a common problem in Vietnam and this hotel eventually overlooked our lack of proof of marriage. Be warned! I had a few beers on my own on New Years day outside the restaurant and one of the girls offered me a free drink for the occasion which was a nice touch. There is a shuttle to downtown that can be booked. We booked it but it was late, so the hotel paid for a taxi for us. Another nice touch! All in all a good place to stay.

reviews.title: Excellent hotel in District 7
reviews.text:
Hotel Ibis Saigon South is value for money accommodation as the room was clean and fuss free. As this was a business trip, the Sweet Bed by Ibis was a feature that I enjoyed and appreciated as it helped ease me into slumber from a tiring day of work. The bed was just right in terms of firmness of mattress, crispness of linen and the cushiness of the quilt. The pillows were a little too soft for my liking, but it was not a bad either. 

However, I find the walls too thin and I can hear people in the corridor and neighbouring room – especially if the guests are from countries that speak loudly. This can be very disturbing especially at night when guests return late (or check in early) and chat loudly. 

In general, I have had a great experience with the Hotel Ibis Saigon South hotel and staff and will not hesitate to recommend it to my friends who need a place to stay in Q7.
reviews.positives: Price, Bed, Internet

reviews.text:
The hotel have clean rooms. The only problem is there is no free mineral water or disposable bedroom slippers in the room. There are 2 Japanese Restaurants next to hotel that serves good food, it is often crowded. There is a convenient store just 1 min walk from the hotel. The Shopping Center is 5 min walk from the hotel where there is a cinema at the top level and a food court at the basement.

The hotel is far from the city center, without traffic, you can get there by taxi in 30min, During rush hours, it'll take about an hour.
The hotel is about 40min taxi from the airport.

reviews.title: Away from the noise of Saigon
reviews.text: We booked a room here for a few days and we were worried as it was located away from most of the attractions. However, it is located near the new modern part of Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon) that most tourists do not usually see. The area is quiet and clean, and there is a few great restaurants loacted across the road.  It's only a quick 15 minute taxi ride to District 1. The staff are friendly and the rooms were clean and modern.
reviews.positives: Location, friendly staff, modern

reviews.title: Good bed, Small room
reviews.text: Stay was fine, but when I tried to have a friend, a legitimate friend who is Vietnamese come and visit me, it and was told she had to pay to stay the night in my room, that I had already paid for! And for the record, she was NOT even staying. She and I were stopping through to drop off stuff, me to take a quick shower and change, and go out for the evening. So IBIS, if you have a policy about letting people staying in your hotel, it needs to be put up front and you need to understand
reviews.positives: The bed, the hot shower, the soap in the bathrooms.

reviews.title: Good, value for money
reviews.text: Ibis is a good place to stay for those who are a little tight on the budget but still willing to stretch themselves for comfort. They have all the BASIC necessities you require in a hotel, including a bottle of dual-purpose soap and shampoo, towels, robes. The area itself, District 7, is a bit far from the city proper. You have to take a cab or ride a bus to Ben Tahn market and such, but it has a good character on its own. Though it's far from the cultural parts of Ho Chi Minh, the area is walking distance from upscale malls. it's a very safe neighbourhood, and there's a grocery store, Family Mart, and Sushi Bar on the ground floor.
reviews.positives: Cleanliness, value for money, full of potential

reviews.title: So Comfortable We Returned for 2nd Stay in 1 Trip
reviews.text:
Purpose of Trip/Stay: Visiting family as well as to travel HCMC
Accompanied By: An aunt
1st Stay: For 2 nights from Nov 15-17
2nd Stay: Another 2 nights from Nov 21-23

Why We Chose to Stay a 2nd Time 
-Affordable: In addition to the reasons already mentioned in my earlier review, price was a big deciding factor for our 2nd stay. With its convenient location & free shuttle service, the hotel is definitely worth a stay if you book at least a few days in advance and clinch the Early special rate.

-Comfortable: We were advised to take the 8th floor instead of 10th like last time due to noise that may arise from top floor renovations (highest floor=13). All I can say is either the reno never took place or we were far enough from it 5 floors down that we never heard it!

-Overall Pleasant: All in all, there isn't much I can complain about after both my stays at the Ibis SS! For my aunt though, believe only 2 things bothered her; and even then, not by much... 
1) Occassional drops in Wifi access although most lasted less than a minute.
2) A/C thermostat was a little off as it got pretty cold at 21.5 deg C, but turn it up to 22 & suddenly the room would get quite abit warmer? We dealt with this by adjusting the temp only right before bed when we can hide under the nice, warm blanket! ;) Cheers!
reviews.positives: Affordable, Comfortable and Overall Pleasant

reviews.title: They really preempt your needs
reviews.text:
I fell asleep in the couch in the inner lobby because I took an early morning flight & was just waiting for the 2:00pm check-in time. The operations manager gently woke me up & informed me I can check in already even if it was just 12:00pm. 

Not only that, because there were noisy concert rehearsals at SECC grounds every night, Ibis made sure they got me a room farthest from the SECC side. I really appreciated that foresight.

reviews.title: Happy Guests
reviews.text:
We are 9 in the group, cousins, bros, sis, nephew.
The hotel was just few mins away from the event (HCMC RUN 2017), exactly where we wanted to be, we loved the view of THE Bridge since we were gnna run there. The front desk staff were all very friendly and speaks English very well, they were able to answer all our question and help us to find locations. Clean hotel and good size of rooms:)

reviews.title: Surprisingly Good
reviews.text: I had some doubts about Ibis initially, but I really enjoyed staying here. The room may be small but well designed. Everything is efficiently placed, so it looks bigger. I love the fact that they use eco-friendly lights and body wash. The staffs can speak English well, definitely very helpful and keen to answer any question. Overall I would stay here again and again.
reviews.positives: Staffs, Food and Services

reviews.title: Nice hotel and excellent service
reviews.text: Quyet place in dictrict 7, good price. The rooms are small but the price and service compensates this.
reviews.positives: Excellent service, clean hotel

reviews.title: Awesome staff, great hotel
reviews.text: I had a great stay here – so great that I booked it for two extra nights, even though I had somewhere else to stay. It's a perfect spot for a business traveler. Strong air con, immaculately clean, brand new/renovated. The only thing I wasn't crazy about were the sheets, they were a bit rough. The staff was really friendly and helpful!
reviews.positives: Clean, newly renovated, nice and helpful staff

reviews.title: Very decent for business trip
reviews.text: The location is very good, suitable if you're attending exhibition in the area. The hotel is wonderful, no complaints.

reviews.title: Concert trip
reviews.text:
Beyond expectations...

1)Front office trainee just assigned my room randomly (I did not request which room) it turned facing Exo Concert stage! Thanks a lot for the best view.

2)The staff responded promptly on request such as iron, new water bottle etc.

3) SECC just nearby; suitable for concertgoer like me.

4) Affordable price, safe  & comfortable similar to other Ibis  in Singapore & Bali.
